codeReview.summary: The feature adds the intended auto-rebase and UI toast behavior, but it does not fully satisfy the required validation contract because the Rust test coverage never exercises the real pull-rebase-push path or conflict-abort behavior. The implementation relies on string-based classification helpers instead of end-to-end ship() tests, leaving the core smart-push flow insufficiently verified.

issues.description: The added tests only cover classify_push_error/classify_rebase_error helpers and serialization, not the actual ship() workflow. The feature explicitly required unit tests for simple push, rebase-then-push, conflict abort, and partial failure, but no test exercises ship() against a repo with a remote, so regressions in the real pull-rebase-push flow can still ship unnoticed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-fullstack skill requires writing failing Rust tests first and its example handoff suggests real ship() smart-push scenarios, but the worker deviated to helper-only tests because realistic repo/remote fixtures were not described. The skill should either document how to create local bare-remotes for git.rs tests or explicitly permit helper-level coverage when end-to-end fixtures are impractical.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file .factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md says 'Write failing tests first' and shows ship_simple_push / ship_rebase_when_behind examples, while the transcript handoff states the worker wrote classification-function tests instead of real ship() tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library does not document a recommended pattern for testing git CLI flows locally with temporary repositories and bare remotes, even though smart-push correctness depends on that capability.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The worker transcript says actual git push/pull tests were skipped due to needing real remotes/credentials, and .factory/library/architecture.md plus user-testing.md contain no guidance for local bare-remote testing of git.rs operations.

summary: Reviewed smart-push commit ef3640d, its handoff, transcript skeleton, and mission/shared-state docs. The implementation appears directionally correct, but I marked the feature failed because the required Rust coverage for the real ship() rebase/push/conflict scenarios is missing; only helper-classification tests were added.
